methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Causaly is a Series B-stage biotech & health software company that has raised $95M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 private peers at the same stage and funding round (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, Profluent) with valuations ranging from $600M to $3.3B, alongside 3 public biotech peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two quantitative methods triangulate to a valuation range. The stage-progression method, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $95M capital raised baseline, implies $180M. The peer-median approach anchors to the $810M median valuation across the 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables. A public-comp P/S anchoring approach (sector median 7.4x) cannot be applied due to absent revenue data for the private target. The triangulated analysis yields a low of $153M, median of $495M, and high of $931M.

The wide dispersion—a 6x spread between low and high—reflects material uncertainty in where Causaly sits within the peer cohort. Stage-progression implies a significantly conservative $180M, while peer-median suggests $810M. The critical unknowns are Causaly's revenue run-rate, growth trajectory relative to the median peer, and sector-specific risk factors. A valuation in the $400M–$700M range appears most defensible pending revenue validation.
